purpose: Publish bounded pre-GitHub-publication readiness criteria so maintainers and reviewers can see why this public specs surface is safe and legible to publish now while keeping traction, adoption, and hosted-runtime claims explicitly deferred.

readiness_families.summary: The repository has a clear front door, community-health files, maintainer identity, and contributor-facing guidance.
readiness_families.why_it_matters: A public repository should be understandable and governable on first contact rather than looking like an internal dump.
bounded_non_claims: does not claim a large contributor community already exists
does not claim post-publication maintainer responsiveness beyond the explicit public intake posture

readiness_families.summary: The public repo has explicit license, notice, boundary, limitations, and portability posture before publication.
readiness_families.why_it_matters: Pre-publish safety depends on clear legal posture and explicit published-vs-deferred boundaries.
bounded_non_claims: does not claim that all upstream N1Hub domains are public here
does not treat publication readiness as proof of hosted-runtime export/import service maturity

readiness_families.summary: The public repo already exposes usable docs, schemas, OpenAPI, examples, and client recipes.
readiness_families.why_it_matters: A publish-ready specs repo should offer concrete consumability, not only governance prose.
bounded_non_claims: does not claim a full SDK or hosted endpoint already exists
does not replace live validator behavior with summary or example layers

readiness_families.summary: Reviewer shortcuts, release evidence, traceability, capability summaries, and program-facing summaries are already explicit and machine-readable.
readiness_families.why_it_matters: Publication readiness improves when outside readers can evaluate the repo quickly without scraping raw history or guessing stronger sources.
bounded_non_claims: does not claim summary layers are stronger than contracts, schemas, provenance, or release evidence
does not claim support-program acceptance from reviewer-readable summaries alone

readiness_families.summary: A single verify entrypoint, repo-local checks, and upstream validator checks make the public surface reviewable before publication.
readiness_families.why_it_matters: A publish-ready repo should have a repeatable pre-publish integrity path instead of one-off manual inspection.
bounded_non_claims: does not claim formal external certification beyond the local verification stack
does not remove the need for deliberate maintainer review before a real GitHub push

readiness_families.summary: The repo can be published now even though external adoption, stars, issues, PR traffic, and support-program outcomes are still future signals.
readiness_families.why_it_matters: Publication readiness should not be confused with already having a mature public operating history.
bounded_non_claims: does not claim external stars, forks, issues, or contributor traffic already exist
does not claim public-hosted-service maturity, user adoption, or support-program approval

non_claims: This file does not claim that the repo has already been published to GitHub.
This file does not claim that publication readiness equals adoption, traction, or acceptance by any support program.
